entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total output of crops and livestock from farming activities.

entities.name: CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental factors such as weather patterns that affect agriculture.

entities.name: GLOBAL POPULATION
entities.description: The total number of humans living on Earth.

entities.name: STRESS-RESILIENT CROPS
entities.description: Crop varieties capable of maintaining productivity under adverse conditions.

entities.name: TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input.

entities.name: STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental factors that negatively impact plant growth and development.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Conventional methods of crop improvement through selection and crossing.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area of land.

entities.name: CRISPR/CAS
entities.description: A precise genome editing tool derived from prokaryotic immune systems.

entities.name: PROKARYOTES
entities.description: Single-celled organisms lacking a nucleus, the origin of CRISPR/Cas.

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: The process of making precise changes to the DNA of a living organism.

entities.name: PLANT MOLECULAR BIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of biological processes in plants at the molecular level.

entities.name: STRESS-TOLERANT CROPS
entities.description: Crops engineered or bred to resist environmental stresses.

entities.name: GENETIC MODIFICATION
entities.description: The alteration of the genetic material of an organism.

entities.name: ALLELIC VARIATIONS
entities.description: Different versions of a gene that occur in a population.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The genetic material of an organism, often stored for breeding.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: The methods used in farming to produce food and fiber.

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: The use of biological systems or living organisms to develop products.

entities.name: PLANT SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY
entities.description: The design and construction of new biological parts or systems in plants.

entities.name: BASE EDITING
entities.description: A CRISPR-derived tool for precise single-nucleotide changes.

entities.name: PRIME EDITING
entities.description: A CRISPR-derived tool for precise insertions, deletions, and all base-to-base conversions.

entities.name: MULTIPLEX GENOME EDITING
entities.description: The simultaneous editing of multiple genomic loci.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME ENGINEERING
entities.description: The manipulation of large-scale chromosomal structures.

entities.name: CROP GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic traits of crops for better performance.

entities.name: CRISPR-MEDIATED GENE DRIVE
entities.description: A system that increases the probability that a specific gene is passed to offspring.

entities.name: TISSUE-SPECIFIC GENOME EDITING
entities.description: Genome editing targeted to specific tissues within a plant.

entities.name: DCAS9 MEDIATED EPIGENETIC MODIFICATION
entities.description: The use of catalytically inactive Cas9 to modify gene expression without changing the DNA sequence.

entities.name: PROGRAMMED SELF-ELIMINATION OF TRANSGENES
entities.description: A technique to remove foreign DNA from a plant after it has served its purpose.

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Multicellular eukaryotic organisms used in agricultural production.
